results.notes: Card name on the card is 'Spider-Byte, Web Warden' (hyphenated); the setup fn is 'spiderbyte_setup' which is why the auto-matcher missed it (name_mismatch). Wired via setup_interceptors=spiderbyte_setup. ETB body is still a placeholder that returns no events because the bounce ability requires player-targeting (engine_gap: targeted-ETB). Helper make_etb_trigger is now correctly registered so once targeting lands the body can be filled in.
results.engine_gap: targeted-ETB selection (return up to one target nonland permanent to its owner's hand)

results.notes: Stub ETB body returns []; wired anyway so the trigger interceptor is at least attached. Two engine gaps remain: (1) targeted graveyard return for a Villain card, (2) a static cost-reduction (Villain spells you cast cost {1} less) which would need a QUERY_COST / cost-modifier interceptor.
results.engine_gap: targeted graveyard-to-hand selection; static cost reduction by subtype (QUERY_COST interceptor)

results.notes: multi_ability case. Existing fn built the menace-attack trigger but never appended it to the returned list (dead code). Fixed by (a) appending the make_attack_trigger via filter_fn=menace_attack_filter, (b) correcting filter signature to (event, state, source) to match helper contract, (c) replacing buggy menace check ('menace' in subtypes or 'creature' in types) with proper attacker.characteristics.keywords lookup. Then wired setup_interceptors=j_jonah_jameson_setup. ETB body remains a placeholder ([]) because 'suspect up to one target creature' needs targeting + a 'suspect' status mechanic that doesn't yet exist.
results.engine_gap: targeted ETB; 'suspect' mechanic (grant menace + can't-block as a status)
